name: Waku
description: Minimal React Server Components framework with file-based routing

name.comment: Official name; ⛩️ the minimal React framework. 'Waku' (わく) means 'framework' in Japanese.

description.value: The minimal React framework — a lightweight, Vite-based framework built around React Server Components
description.comment: Value proposition from homepage; targets marketing sites, headless commerce, and small/medium web apps.

license.comment: SPDX MIT per GitHub license metadata.

category.comment: Owns its build/server toolchain (Vite + Hono); routing is one part of a full framework.

active-maintenance.comment: Repo last pushed 2026-07-15; 1.0 beta released mid-2026. Actively developed by Daishi Kato. Checked 2026-07-15.

initial-release.comment: Repo created 2023-03-03; first public announcement was v0.10.0 on 2023-05-22.

latest-release.comment: v1.0.0-beta.6, released 2026-06-28. Waku 1.0 reached beta (May 2026); the 1.0 line is not yet marked fully stable. Checked 2026-07-15.

npm-weekly-downloads.comment: Last-week downloads of the 'waku' package (Jul 8-14, 2026). Checked 2026-07-15.

routing-paradigm.comment: Default file-based pages router (./src/pages) plus a programmatic config-object API via createPages.

nested-layout-routes.comment: Nested routes with shared _layout.tsx layouts; group (parentheses) folders share layouts without affecting URLs.

dynamic-params.comment: Dynamic segments via [slug] bracket notation, passed as props.

optional-catch-all-params.comment: Catch-all routes via [...catchAll] receive an array of path segments.

search-params-api.comment: useRouter exposes query; structured navigation supports search objects: router.push({ to, search: { q } }).

programmatic-navigation.comment: useRouter() provides push, replace, reload, back, forward, prefetch.

route-guards-middleware.comment: Hono-based middleware (./src/middleware) and handler interceptors (_interceptors) wrap renders/API handlers for auth, logging, request scoping.

written-in-typescript.comment: Authored in TypeScript (~94% of repo); ships first-class type definitions.

type-safe-paths-links.comment: Generated pages.gen.ts gives type checks and autocompletion for <Link to> and useRouter().push() paths; invalid paths are compile errors.

type-safe-params.comment: PageProps<'/blog/[slug]'> infers typed path params for dynamic segments.

type-safe-search-params.comment: Query is exposed as a plain string; no schema-based typing/validation of search params (unlike TanStack Router).

codegen-required.comment: Type-safe routing relies on a generated pages.gen.ts file (checked into VCS), not pure inference.

type-safety-rating.comment: Typed paths/links and typed path params via codegen, but search params are not schema-typed/validated — matches the 'typed params or links with gaps' tier.

route-data-loaders.comment: No loader/useLoaderData primitive; data is fetched directly inside async React Server Components rather than a route-level loader.

actions-mutations.comment: First-class React server actions/functions callable directly from components without manual API wiring.

prefetching.comment: router.prefetch(path) and Link prefetching for route code/data.

pending-transition-ui.comment: Integrates with React 19 useTransition and useActionState for pending/transition states during navigation and actions.

scroll-restoration.comment: Scroll restoration is not explicitly documented; could not confirm a built-in feature.

per-route-code-splitting.comment: Server components keep heavy deps out of client bundles; slices/lazy slices load on demand with per-page splitting.

per-route-error-handling.comment: A default error boundary exists at the app root and server errors replay to the client; no dedicated per-route error-element primitive is documented (custom boundaries must be placed manually in layouts).

streaming-ssr.comment: RSC streaming (rsc-html-stream dependency); homepage cites progressive/streaming rendering.

react-server-components.comment: RSC is the core purpose of Waku; built specifically to explore and productionize React Server Components.

static-site-generation.comment: Static prerendering via getConfig render: 'static' with staticPaths.

client-only-usable.comment: RSC-first framework requiring its own build/server toolchain; not a pure CSR SPA router you drop into any React app.

framework-bound.comment: Requires its own Vite + Hono toolchain and build; not a drop-in router for an arbitrary React app.

bundle-size.comment: Meta-framework — a single 'router bundle' figure isn't meaningful; framework runtime size isn't comparable to a standalone router.

zero-runtime-dependencies.comment: Ships runtime deps including hono, vite, @vitejs/plugin-rsc, rsc-html-stream, magic-string, dotenv, picocolors.

tree-shakeable.comment: Server components tree-shake server-only code out of client bundles; per-page/slice code splitting.

documentation-quality.comment: Adequate, concise docs plus a practices guide and a steady blog covering routing, RSC, SSR/SSG, server actions and type safety; some areas thin, consistent with a minimal framework still stabilizing at 1.0.

official-devtools.comment: No dedicated route/navigation devtools found in docs or repo.

data-fetching-integration.comment: Primary data fetching is via async React Server Components (RSC); docs note compatibility with client libraries like React Query.

predecessor-lineage.comment: No predecessor router; an original framework by Daishi Kato (author of Zustand/Jotai/Valtio), created to explore RSC on Vite.
